prompt:
This query behind our orders listing endpoint takes ~6s and gets worse every week. Can you review it?

```sql
SELECT *
FROM orders o
LEFT JOIN order_item i ON i.order_id = o.id
LEFT JOIN payment pg ON pg.order_id = o.id
LEFT JOIN customer c ON c.id = o.customer_id
WHERE DATE(o.created_at) >= '2025-01-01'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC
LIMIT 20 OFFSET 80000;
```

The `orders` table already has ~40 million rows and grows every day. `order_item` and `payment` are 1:N per order.

assertions.text: Identifies the cartesian/row-explosion risk from the 1:N joins (order_item and payment) and suggests separate queries or aggregation
Flags DATE(o.created_at) as a function/cast on the column that disables the index, and rewrites it as a range on the raw column
Flags SELECT * and recommends selecting only the needed columns
Flags the high OFFSET (80000) and recommends keyset/cursor pagination
States a tradeoff for at least one recommendation (not just the fix)
Provides at least one corrected SQL example
